behavior_summary: U8P_DEMNSTAT::look is a simple U8P_DEMNSTAT::look/inspect handler that displays a short descriptive text identifying the object as a 'demon statue'.
After emitting the single bark/text the process suspends, ending the U8P_DEMNSTAT::look interaction without modifying game state or calling external systems.
No menus, branching, flags, or external calls are present; the routine only provides a player-facing description when examined.
notable_control_flow: U8P_DEMNSTAT::look: emit bark 'demon statue ' -> suspend (end of process).
Execution is linear and unbranched; there are two unknown/decompiler remnants but they do not affect observable behavior.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the text 'demon statue ' intentional (formatting concatenation elsewhere) or an artifact?
Might there be other processes or handlers for this object (use/activate) defined in other files that extend behavior?
Are the two Unknown nodes in the function purely decompiler remnants or do they indicate a wrapper/block structure used by the engine?

main_function.name: U8P_DEMNSTAT::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description shown to the player when the object is examined; then suspend/terminate the U8P_DEMNSTAT::look process.

May also run when the player or actor enters a U8P_DEMNSTAT::look-type fast-area trigger for this object (inferred).
